Politics.headline: US takes Iran-flagged ship into custody, Trump says
Politics.detail: President Trump announced that U.S. forces seized an Iranian-flagged ship attempting to bypass the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, marking the first ship boarding since the naval blockade took effect.

Politics.headline: Trump says Iran agrees to hand over 'nuclear dust'
Politics.detail: President Trump claimed that Iran has agreed to hand over its enriched uranium, but Iran disputes claims of new agreements with Trump.

Politics.headline: DOJ demands Detroit-area 2024 ballots, escalating election scrutiny
Politics.detail: The Justice Department, led by Assistant Attorney General Harmeet K. Dhillon, is demanding 2024 ballots from the Detroit area to investigate election law compliance, a move that Democratic state officials are calling baseless.

Politics.headline: Trump joined by Joe Rogan as he signs order to speed up psychedelic review
Politics.detail: President Trump, with Joe Rogan present, signed an order to expedite the review process for psychedelics.

Politics.headline: Ontario government buys $28.9M private jet for Doug Ford's use
Politics.detail: The Ontario government is looking to sell the $28.9 million private jet that was recently purchased for Premier Doug Ford's use "as quickly as possible," following backlash days after the purchase was confirmed publicly.

Finance & Economy.headline: Financial markets fall to new 2026 low as oil rises again
Finance & Economy.detail: The ongoing Iran war is impacting the global economy, according to a new IMF forecast, causing financial markets to decline.

Finance & Economy.headline: Stock Market Futures Down 1%
Finance & Economy.detail: Stock market futures were down nearly 1%, pointing to a weaker open for US equities as risk sentiment deteriorates amid renewed geopolitical tensions.

Finance & Economy.headline: New Zealand Trade Surplus Beats Forecasts
Finance & Economy.detail: New Zealand posted a NZD 0.70 billion trade surplus in March 2026, shifting from a deficit a year earlier and beating estimates, as both exports and imports increased.

Finance & Economy.headline: Fed’s Beige Book reveals Iran conflict’s impact on business confidence.
Finance & Economy.detail: The Federal Reserve's Beige Book indicates that the conflict in Iran is affecting business confidence in the US.

Finance & Economy.headline: Carney's pitch to unlock trillions in global investment
Finance & Economy.detail: Prime Minister Mark Carney is hosting an Invest in Canada Summit this fall, inviting 100 of the biggest investment firms to unlock trillions in investments in Canada.

Technology.headline: Germany's DLR Flies Morphing Wing
Technology.detail: The German aerospace center DLR has begun flying an uncrewed testbed to demonstrate morphing-wing technology in flight under the MorphAIR project, utilizing the Proteus test aircraft. The project aims to research morphing technologies and artificial intelligence in the aerospace industry.

Technology.headline: ITRI Earns Five 2026 Edison Awards
Technology.detail: The Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI) was recognized at the 2026 Edison Awards for five breakthrough technologies, including LigamiX™ for ligament repair, Sustainable Pavement Material Recycling, and Prognosis Monitoring System (PMS). ITRI won three gold and two silver awards, ranking second globally in total number of awards.

Technology.headline: SA 'urban mining' tech reaches milestone
Technology.detail: Iondrive is developing IONSolv, an 'urban mining platform' to extract valuable metals from e-waste, batteries, solar panels, and mining tailings. Recent testing in the United States showed that IONSolv is delivering consistent high extraction, which supports the company's transition toward a scalable extraction platform.

Technology.headline: Tech sector faces reality check
Technology.detail: Analysts are downgrading earnings expectations for Malaysian tech companies, with semiconductor names revised down by around 9% to 11% and EMS players by as much as 30%, suggesting previous expectations were too optimistic. Customer concentration remains a structural vulnerability as many Malaysian tech companies rely on a small number of global clients.

Technology.headline: AI agents could pose security risks
Technology.detail: Cybersecurity experts are raising concerns about the security risks associated with AI 'agents' that automate tasks, like OpenClaw, which has over three million users. These systems, powered by large language models, could be exploited for financial gain or other nefarious purposes.

Science.headline: Shanghai firm promotes radar tech use in healthcare products
Science.detail: Shanghai Hecaray Technology has developed non-contact health monitoring products based on millimeter-wave radar technologies, including sleep monitors, vital signs monitors, and fall alarms, with all components made in China. These products have been sold to over 20 countries and regions.

Science.headline: Hollywood, Silicon Valley turn out for 'Oscars of Science'
Science.detail: Big names from film, tech, music, and sports gathered in Santa Monica for the Breakthrough Prizes, which recognize research achievements of leading scientists in Life Sciences, Fundamental Physics, and Mathematics. The awards, co-founded by philanthropists and tech entrepreneurs, highlight the importance of scientific work.

Science.headline: Science Reveals 6 Ways Smartwatches Mislead Users
Science.detail: Smartwatches can be inaccurate in tracking calories burned, heart rate during intense exercise, and VO2 max, potentially misleading users about their fitness levels. These inaccuracies stem from estimation methods based on heart rate and movement rather than direct measurement.

Science.headline: NASA Rolls Out “Top Four-Fifths” of Artemis III SLS Rocket
Science.detail: NASA is rolling out the “top four-fifths” of the Artemis III SLS rocket at the Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans. This is in preparation for the Artemis III mission, which aims to send humans back to the Moon.

Science.headline: New study links largest teen cannabis use with slower cognitive development
Science.detail: A new study indicates that the largest cannabis use among teenagers is linked to slower cognitive development. Researchers are exploring the effects of cannabis on the cognitive functions of adolescents.

Health.headline: Applications now open for Ahpra Board vacancies
Health.detail: The Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency (Ahpra) is seeking applications from community members with expertise in health, education, business administration, or regulation to serve on the Ahpra Board and help ensure a culturally safe and responsive health workforce. Applications close on May 10, 2026.

Health.headline: Grey-market peptides: an emerging public health challenge
Health.detail: Clinicians are seeing more patients using unregulated injectable peptide-based substances, promoted online for weight loss, and should be aware of the risks. Australian clinicians are increasingly encountering patients using or inquiring about unregulated injectable peptide-based substances, and there is concern about the number of patients in obesity clinics using these products.

Health.headline: Trump issues more threats ahead of talks | Morning News Bulletin 20 April 2026
Health.detail: The federal government in Australia is introducing free Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) vaccinations for older Australians starting May 15 for those 75 and over, and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people from age 60, to protect them from the virus. The Health Minister also recommends that vulnerable Australians access free influenza and COVID-19 vaccine programs as winter approaches.

Health.headline: Smoothies vs juice: study reveals which fruit form is healthiest
Health.detail: A study of 443 adults found that fruit smoothies and solid fruits are associated with better overall health outcomes compared to fruit juices. However, the findings reflect correlations rather than causation and may be influenced by lifestyle differences among participants.

Health.headline: Early Warning Signs of Diabetes
Health.detail: Diabetes affects millions and often goes undiagnosed, so it is important to discover how it develops, recognize common symptoms, and learn ways to stay in control.

Sports.headline: Shane Flanagan sacked as Dragons coach
Sports.detail: NRL team, the Dragons, have sacked their coach Shane Flanagan after a string of losses; the club confirmed that General Manager of Football Ben Haran has also departed.

Sports.headline: Fitzpatrick survives Scheffler playoff to win RBC Heritage
Sports.detail: Matt Fitzpatrick survived a playoff against Scottie Scheffler to win the RBC Heritage tournament.

Sports.headline: Bulldogs join forces with NSW Government to tackle sideline abuse
Sports.detail: The Canterbury Bankstown Bulldogs are supporting a NSW Government campaign to promote positive sideline behavior at kids' sporting events.

Sports.headline: Davies scores 1st goal in over a year to help Bayern Munich clinch Bundesliga title
Sports.detail: Alphonso Davies scored his first goal in over a year, contributing to Bayern Munich's Bundesliga title win.

Sports.headline: Rinku, Anukul combine to script KKR's first win
Sports.detail: Rinku and Anukul contributed significantly to Kolkata Knight Riders' (KKR) first win in the IPL 2026 season.

Entertainment.headline: Justin Bieber Coachella 2026 Weekend 2 Highlights: Billie Eilish 'One Less Lonely Girl' & More
Entertainment.detail: Justin Bieber returned to the stage at Coachella 2026, and Billie Eilish joined him for a rendition of 'One Less Lonely Girl.' The music festival was really Bieberchella for all the Beliebers.

Entertainment.headline: Is Justin Bieber Going on Tour? What We Know After Coachella 2026 Performance
Entertainment.detail: After Justin Bieber's surprise appearance at Coachella 2026, fans are speculating about a potential tour. Here's what we currently know about the possibility of Justin Bieber going on tour.

Entertainment.headline: Kyra Sedgwick Reveals She & Husband Kevin Bacon Have Had Sex in Movie Set Trailers
Entertainment.detail: Kyra Sedgwick shared a surprising detail about her marriage with Kevin Bacon, revealing that they have had sexual encounters in movie set trailers during their careers.

Entertainment.headline: TWICE reflect on a decade as a group: 'There's so much more we can do' | AP interview
Entertainment.detail: TWICE members Nayeon, Jeongyeon, Momo, Sana, Jihyo, Mina, Dahyun, Chaeyoung, and Tzuyu discuss their anniversary release, evolving definitions of success and balancing solo ambitions.

Entertainment.headline: Damon Albarn and Jamie Hewlett on 'The Mountain' and building the Gorillaz | AP interview
Entertainment.detail: Damon Albarn and Jamie Hewlett discuss their creative process, the virtual band's return and the role of collaboration in shaping the project.

Crime & Justice.headline: 8 children killed in Louisiana shooting, gunman fatally shot by police
Crime & Justice.detail: A Louisiana community is in shock after a domestic violence incident leaves eight children and two others injured; the gunman was fatally shot by police.

Crime & Justice.headline: 5 wounded in shooting near University of Iowa, including 3 students
Crime & Justice.detail: Officers from the Iowa City Police Department heard gunfire while responding to a report of a large fight, the department said.

Crime & Justice.headline: Haiti's Culture Ministry fires workers over citadel stampede that killed 25
Crime & Justice.detail: At least nine people have been arrested following the stampede, including police officers and ministry employees.

Crime & Justice.headline: Rapper d4vd arrested on suspicion of murdering 14-year-old girl
Crime & Justice.detail: David Burke, a singer who performs under the name d4vd, is behind bars on suspicion of murder after the body of a 14-year-old girl was found in the trunk of his Tesla in September 2025.

Crime & Justice.headline: Israeli strike on Gaza police car kills child
Crime & Justice.detail: An Israeli airstrike on a police vehicle in Gaza City killed at least four people, including a three-year-old child.

Education.headline: Zero dropout policy draws praise
Education.detail: Thailand's zero dropout policy is gaining international recognition for its commitment to inclusive and equitable quality education, aiming to ensure all children have untapped potential. The approach drew international attention at a recent forum hosted by the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (Unesco) in Paris.

Education.headline: Google hiring for Gemini for Education roles in higher ed partnerships
Education.detail: Google is seeking individuals to drive the adoption of Gemini for Education within higher education institutions. These roles require experience working with C-level executives and navigating complex environments, placing Gemini for Education at the core of Google's academic strategy.

Education.headline: Online education market
Education.detail: The global digital education market is projected to reach $134 billion in revenue by 2030, according to Grand View Research. This estimate was featured in Business Insider.

Education.headline: Today News Headlines for School Assembly, April 20, 2026: Daily LPG demand drops below 50 lakh, Iran dismisses Trump's war claims, and more
Education.detail: Mumbai is preparing to host foreign universities planning to open campuses in 2026, and the Maharashtra government is reinforcing its language policy by warning schools they risk losing recognition if they don't teach Marathi. These headlines are curated for students, teachers, and morning assemblies, covering national, international, sports, business, and GK current affairs.

Education.headline: U.S. Secretary of Education Linda McMahon Honors Louisiana Custodian Donella Wagner as 2026 RISE Award Honoree
Education.detail: U.S. Secretary of Education Linda McMahon named Donella Wagner, Head Custodian at Raintree Elementary School in Baldwin, Louisiana, as the 2026 National Recognizing Inspiring School Employees (RISE) Award honoree. The U.S. Department of Education is committed to fostering educational excellence and ensuring equal access.

Lifestyle.headline: 90-day challenge to promote healthy lifestyle among civil servants
Lifestyle.detail: The Health Ministry in Malaysia has launched a 90-day 'Healthy Steps Challenge' to encourage a more active and healthier lifestyle among civil servants, addressing concerns raised by the National Health and Morbidity Survey about physical inactivity and sedentary lifestyles. The initiative is part of a broader effort to drive behavioral change and promote well-being.

Lifestyle.headline: Skincare in your 30s: Spending just enough, but splurging a little
Lifestyle.detail: This article likely discusses skincare routines and product recommendations for individuals in their 30s, focusing on balancing cost-effectiveness with investing in quality products.

Lifestyle.headline: Breaking silence in Newa beats
Lifestyle.detail: Ujjwala Maharjan is using rap and spoken word to reimagine Newa culture while confronting questions of gender and belonging. Her work breaks silence and explores identity through music.

Lifestyle.headline: TaoKaeNoi x Chen Zheyuan taps Chinese star power to boost seaweed snack growth
Lifestyle.detail: TaoKaeNoi is collaborating with Chinese star Chen Zheyuan to promote its seaweed snacks and evolve the brand into a global lifestyle brand, focusing on providing consumers with 'guilt-free indulgence' experiences. This reflects a shift towards lifestyle-driven snack consumption.

Lifestyle.headline: Actress Christy Chung and husband Shawn Zhang considering having a 4th child
Lifestyle.detail: Actress Christy Chung and her husband, Shawn Zhang, are reportedly considering having a fourth child. This follows reports of Malaysian star Fish Liew winning Best Actress at an awards show.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Alliance building as a security strategy
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Philippines and its allies are increasing security partnerships to counter China's aggressive moves in the Asia-Pacific region, emphasizing a commitment to a stable and peaceful region.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Long-suffering general budget freed
Opinion/Editorial.detail: After prolonged political gridlock, the Legislative Yuan and Cabinet reached a consensus to review this year's general budget, potentially ending a 230-day delay. The Cabinet has agreed to implement a portion of the budget selected by the opposition for new initiatives.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Letters to The Editor — April 20, 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The letters discuss various issues, including the defeat of the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, with one letter criticizing the Prime Minister's address to the nation regarding the opposition's rejection of the bill.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Our seas, our stories
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The article reflects on the importance of museums in sharing stories and discoveries, highlighting the often unacknowledged talent within cultural institutions and the upcoming gathering at the Museo del Galeón.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: First island chain teamwork key
Opinion/Editorial.detail: In the face of escalating military pressure from China, nations along the First Island Chain must work together to defend themselves and respond to threats, making the chain a major front line for global freedom and democracy.

World.headline: US military seized Iranian-flagged container ship, Trump says
World.detail: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the U.S. military seized an Iranian-flagged container ship in the Gulf of Oman after it attempted to bypass a naval blockade, leading to heightened tensions.

World.headline: Mass shooting rampage in Louisiana leaves eight children dead and others wounded
World.detail: A mass shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana, resulted in the deaths of eight children and injuries to others, described by police as a domestic violence incident.

World.headline: Iran-Israel war updates: No plan for Tehran to attend new talks with U.S. in Pakistan, says Iranian media
World.detail: Despite earlier indications, Iranian media reported that Tehran has no plans to participate in new talks with the U.S. in Pakistan, while tensions remain high over the U.S. naval blockade.

World.headline: Images show Israel building permanent military bases in Gaza as US-backed reconstruction plans stall
World.detail: Reports and images indicate that Israel is constructing permanent military bases in Gaza, coinciding with the stalling of US-backed reconstruction plans.

World.headline: Bulgaria's former president claims 'victory' after projections show him winning parliamentary elections
World.detail: Rumen Radev, Bulgaria's former president, claimed victory after projections indicated his party would win the parliamentary elections, potentially leading to a shift in the country's foreign policy.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mnangagwa pledges faster road rehabilitation, rural electrification
Zimbabwe.detail: President Emmerson Mnangagwa says the government is intensifying road rehabilitation and rural electrification programs as part of efforts to modernize infrastructure and drive economic development. He made these remarks during Independence Day celebrations.

Zimbabwe.headline: Vendors strike gold at Independence celebrations
Zimbabwe.detail: Informal traders and small-scale entrepreneurs seized the opportunity presented by Zimbabwe's 46th Independence Day celebrations in Maphisa, Matabeleland South, turning the national event into a bustling marketplace. They sold food, drinks and other goods to thousands of attendees.

Zimbabwe.headline: Drone scheme to speed medical supplies to remote areas
Zimbabwe.detail: A new drone delivery initiative launched by Drone Solutions is set to transform how critical medical supplies reach remote communities in Zimbabwe, positioning the country as a growing hub for high-tech healthcare logistics in the region. The initiative aims to improve access to essential medicines and supplies in remote areas.

Zimbabwe.headline: CCC faction under Tshabangu in covert rapprochement with Mnangagwa regime
Zimbabwe.detail: Fresh allegations have emerged suggesting that a faction of the Citizens Coalition for Change (CCC), led by Sengezo Tshabangu, is engaging in covert rapprochement with the Emmerson Mnangagwa regime. This comes amid intensifying power struggles and cracks within ZANU-PF.

Zimbabwe.headline: Highlanders coach Mwaruwari owed 3 months' salary as Chivayo payment chain breaks down
Zimbabwe.detail: Highlanders coach Benjani Mwaruwari has not been paid for three months due to a breakdown in the payment chain involving Wicknell Chivayo, who had pledged to support the team. This situation highlights the financial challenges faced by some Zimbabwean football clubs.
